program_name: Home and Community Care Support Services (HCCSS)
eligibility_summary: OHIP-insured resident with a care need identified through the single point of access; priority for post-acute, end of life, and complex chronic cases
user_fee_model: No user fee for publicly-funded services within HCCSS cap
scope_includes: Personal support, nursing, OT/PT, palliative, end-of-life, meal prep
scope_excludes: Housekeeping beyond minimal, companion care, 24-hour supervision
notes: Formerly Community Care Access Centres (CCAC, 1996–2016) and LHIN Home and Community Care (2016–2021)

eligibility_summary: Universal access for Quebec residents with demonstrated need; priority for OEMC-assessed high-risk seniors
user_fee_model: No fee for direct CLSC services; user fee possible for services purchased via EESAD social economy enterprises
scope_includes: Personal care, nursing, rehab, housekeeping via EESAD, meal services
notes: Quebec's subsidised hours are among the highest in Canada for high-need clients

administering_body: Five regional Health Authorities (Fraser, Vancouver Coastal, Interior, Island, Northern)
eligibility_summary: BC resident with a care need; priority based on assessed acuity
user_fee_model: Client contribution based on after-tax income; subsidised above income thresholds
scope_includes: Personal care, nursing, rehab, adult day programs
scope_excludes: Housekeeping and meal preparation (not publicly funded in BC)
notes: BC explicitly excludes housekeeping from publicly funded home care — gap that families often fill with private services

administering_body: Alberta Health Services (AHS) Continuing Care division
eligibility_summary: Alberta resident assessed as meeting continuing care eligibility
user_fee_model: No fee for nursing; accommodation and support services subject to cost-share
notes: Alberta's 2022 Continuing Care Act consolidated previous home care legislation

eligibility_summary: Saskatchewan resident with assessed care need
user_fee_model: Income-tested fee for personal care and homemaking; nursing free
notes: Saskatchewan is one of the few provinces with funded homemaking alongside personal care

eligibility_summary: Manitoba resident with a Provincial Health Insurance Card and assessed need
user_fee_model: No fee — universal access for eligible residents
scope_includes: Personal care, home support, nursing, respite
notes: Manitoba has Canada's longest-standing universal home care program (est. 1974)

program_name: Nova Scotia Continuing Care Home Care
administering_body: Department of Health and Wellness, delivered via Nova Scotia Health
eligibility_summary: Nova Scotia resident with assessed need through single entry access
user_fee_model: Income-tested fee for home support; nursing and professional services free
scope_includes: Personal care, home support, nursing, rehab, end-of-life

eligibility_summary: NB resident requiring acute, rehab, palliative, or chronic care at home
user_fee_model: No fee for medically necessary services
scope_includes: Nursing, rehab, palliative, respiratory therapy, IV therapy
scope_excludes: Housekeeping (provided by separate Long-Term Care Services Home Support program)
notes: Personal care and home support delivered through a separate Social Development program

program_name: Newfoundland and Labrador Home Support Program
eligibility_summary: NL resident with assessed care need and financial eligibility
user_fee_model: Financially assessed; co-payment on a sliding scale

eligibility_summary: PEI resident with assessed care need
user_fee_model: Universal access to nursing; home support income-tested

administering_body: Yukon Department of Health and Social Services
eligibility_summary: Yukon resident with assessed care need
scope_excludes: Limited weekend and evening capacity in rural communities
notes: Provider capacity is the binding constraint outside Whitehorse

administering_body: NWT Department of Health and Social Services
eligibility_summary: NWT resident with assessed care need
scope_includes: Personal care, homemaking, nursing (where staffing available)
scope_excludes: Service availability is highly dependent on community staffing

administering_body: Government of Nunavut, Department of Health
eligibility_summary: Nunavut resident with assessed care need
scope_excludes: Service scope varies dramatically by community
notes: Most remote and most capacity-constrained program in Canada

eligibility_summary: Eligible veterans and primary caregivers; survivors under specified conditions
user_fee_model: Reimbursement of documented expenses up to annual ceilings
scope_includes: Housekeeping, grounds maintenance, personal care, nutrition, professional health and home adaptation
scope_excludes: Services not approved in the VAC care plan
notes: Reimbursement model; see home_care_tax_parameters_2026.csv for approximate annual ceilings

program_name: FNIHB Home and Community Care Program
administering_body: Indigenous Services Canada — First Nations and Inuit Health Branch
eligibility_summary: Status First Nations and recognised Inuit community residents
scope_includes: Personal care, home support, nursing, respite, community care
notes: Scope and staffing capacity vary substantially by community
